title: terminalia bellirica var. laurinoides
intro: Terminalia bellirica var. laurinoides is a variant of the Terminalia bellirica plant, commonly known as the "black myrobalan" or "bahera", which is a type of tree native to the Indian subcontinent. This plant has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Terminalia bellirica var. laurinoides include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to make medicinal teas, capsules, or topical applications for skin and wound care.
